reasoning: The station is in Gourdon, France. The daily average temperature on October 26 is historically around 12-13°C, whereas on January 24 (the assumed resolution date) the mean is around 5°C. For January to be warmer than a randomly drawn October 26 requires either an unseasonably cold October day, an unusually warm January day, or both. Given standard deviations around 3.5°C for both months, the Z-score for the difference is roughly -1.4 to -1.5, suggesting a ~8% chance that the January date will be warmer than the October date.
